direction.current_focus: Define a small world foundation brief so canon content can be written on purpose before any simulation work starts.
direction.why_now: The board pointed at simulation too early. The next clean dependency is collaborative world design: premise, tone, tensions, reality rules, era, and the first important people, groups, and places.

items.title: Finish exact Hermes builder-memory adoption and remove any remaining fake builder-memory sources.
items.why: Builder continuity has to be exact and singular before the separate world systems are designed.
items.done_when: The vendored Hermes memory module is the only builder-memory implementation and the tests stay clean.

items.title: Keep one simple todo board that stays truthful as future slices land.
items.why: Planning drift is still how the repo gets sloppy again, even after the rebuild is cleaner.
items.done_when: After each clean stop, the board still has one active slice, a clear next horizon, and a clear later horizon without hidden thread context.

items.title: Define the canon engine boundary so world truth stays separate from operator memory and runtime beliefs.
items.why: World continuity is still unsolved until canon has its own home.
items.done_when: Canon facts, approvals, and runtime beliefs have separate boundaries.

items.title: Build the simulation runtime that reads canon without overwriting it.
items.why: The living world should emerge from canon instead of replacing it.
items.done_when: Runtime state can evolve scene by scene while canon stays authoritative.

items.title: Define the world foundation before any simulation work: premise, tone, tensions, reality rules, era, and the first important people, groups, and places.
items.why: Canon needs a strong foundation before the repo starts storing world content or shaping a living runtime.
items.done_when: A small world-foundation brief exists, reflects collaborative decisions, and leaves some areas intentionally unknown.

items.title: Turn the world foundation into the first small canon content slice.
items.why: Once the foundation is clear, the next step is approved world facts, not simulation behavior.
items.done_when: The first canon entities, places, groups, rules, and timeline anchors are drafted in a reviewable shape.

items.title: Build the player-facing layer for entering the living world.
items.why: The user insertion layer only makes sense after canon and simulation are stable.
items.done_when: The system can place the user into a persistent scene without confusing builder state with world state.
